MDDescription: Most functionality is accessed through chat commands. Simply type a command in the chat box, just as you would when chatting with teammates.
>Note: Remember to use the Allies or All chat channel when entering commands. Public chat channels and Private Messages (PM) do not work.
All commands have a short alias with identical functionality, allowing for less typing.
Some commands have a UI counterpart that displays a menu of options and features corresponding to the command's functionality.

_metadata.libraryDescription: A unified module loader library for selecting which modules to load.
_metadata.overrideMarkdownContent: This module is for loading other modules. **DO NOT** modify this file in SC2Editor.

_metadata.libraryDescription: A library providing utility tools such as "Toggle Fog Of War", "Toggle UI", etc.

description: Toggle Mass CDR. Resets the cooldown on all abilities every game loop (0.0625s).
This is useful for bypassing the limit of Try Mode's Toggle Cooldown, which has a 1.5s cooldown between each reset.

examples.description: Toggle Mass CDR
Toggle Mass CDR

description: Toggle random movement for selected units.
This command creates a circle centered on your camera. Selected units will begin moving randomly within the circle.
This is useful for practicing various things, such as skillshots.
Run this command again to turn off this functionality.

>Tip: To get the most out of this tool, use [`alliancecontrolteamselection`](#cmd-alliancecontrolteamselection) to select desired units, then use [summon units](#cmd-summon) to let them move randomly.
>Tip: Also use the [`addbehavior`](#cmd-addbehavior) command to add the `Mounted` (`adb Mounted`) behavior for faster movement (mount speed), and `adb DisableWeaponsBehavior` to disable auto attacks.
>Note: You can still issue commands to units that are moving randomly, but once all commands are finished (e.g., the unit stops), it will resume moving randomly within the circle.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The radius of the circle that units will be moved randomly

examples.description: Toggle Random Movement with circle radius of 10.
Toggle Random Movement with circle radius of default 7.

description: Add a marker at the position of the first selected unit.

parameters.name: MarkerID
parameters.description: Defines the Marker ID to be added (up to 20).

examples.description: Add marker 1 at the position of the first selected unit.
Add marker 10 at the position of the first selected unit.

description: Add a marker at the center position of the camera.
>Tip: Also use [`togglecrosshair`](#cmd-togglecrosshair) command to set the location precisely.

parameters.name: MarkerID
parameters.description: Defines the Marker ID to be added (up to 20).

examples.description: Add marker 1 at the center position of the camera.
Add marker 10 at the center position of the camera.

description: Get a Markers position in (X, Y, Z).

parameters.name: MarkerID
parameters.description: Defines the Marker ID to be added (up to 20).

examples.description: Get the position of Marker 1 in (X, Y, Z).
Get the position of Marker 10 in (X, Y, Z).

description: Change the color of a specific marker using an RGB value. The new color also applies to future markers.
>Note: Each marker's color is generated randomly when the game starts.

parameters.name: MarkerID
parameters.description: Defines the Marker ID to be added (up to 20).

parameters.name: Red
parameters.description: Defines the Red color of the marker's RGB color. (0 - 255)

parameters.name: Green
parameters.description: Defines the Green color of the marker's RGB color. (0 - 255)

parameters.name: Blue
parameters.description: Defines the Blue color of the marker's RGB color. (0 - 255)

examples.description: Change Marker 1 color to 255, 0, 0  (Red)
Change Marker 10 color to 0, 0, 0  (Black)
Change Marker 10 color to 0, 255, 255  (Cyan)

parameters.name: MarkerID
parameters.description: Defines the Marker ID to be added (up to 20).

examples.description: Removed Marker 1
Removed Marker 10
Clear all created markers.
Clear all created markers.

commands.description: Toggle a crosshair at the center of the camera. This allows for more precise placement of [summon units](#cmd-summon) and [adding effects](#cmd-addeffectcamera).

examples.description: Toggle showing crosshair
Toggle showing crosshair

commands.description: Toggle Debug Mode by modifying the `libCore_gv_dEBUGDebuggingEnabled` variable.

examples.description: Toggle Debug Mode
Toggle Debug Mode

commands.description: Toggle Fog Of War. This can enable or disable full map vision.

examples.description: Toggle Full Map Vision
Toggle Full Map Vision

description: Toggle the UI. This hides or shows all UI elements, including the HP bar, talents panel, floating combat text, abilities bar, etc.
>Note: When the UI is off, all abilities are disabled.

examples.description: Toggle In game UI
Toggle In game UI

commands.description: Clear all text messages, including chat and debug messages with an optional delay.

parameters.name: seconds
parameters.description: Defines the delay to clear text messages in seconds.

examples.description: Clear all texts instantly
Clear all texts after 5.4 seconds

description: Restart the map without needing to re-enter. This is extremely useful when testing modified XML files or Galaxy scripts.
>Note: It will **NOT** automatically reload or add mods from `Includes.xml`. You must manually re-enter the map for mods to be applied.

examples.description: Restart the game instantly
Restart the game instantly

commands.description: Start the game instantly (timer start, gate open, etc).

examples.description: Start the game instantly
Start the game instantly

commands.description: End the game instantly and kick all players from the game.

examples.description: End the game instantly
End the game instantly

description: Instantly declare a win for a team and end the game.
Generally left team is **Team 1** and right team is **Team 2**.

parameters.name: TeamID
parameters.description: Defines the team ID to win the game.

examples.description: Win the game for Team 1
Win the game for Team 2

description: Remove the camera bounding box, which is defined in the map editor. This allows the camera to move freely without being limited to a specific area.
>Note: This command cannot be reverted. You will need to restart the game to get the bounding box back.

examples.description: Remove the camera bounding box
Remove the camera bounding box

description: Manually set the camera object. The parameter is the id of `<CCamera id="xxx">`.
>**WARNING**: Set a non existing camera will actually **crash** the game.

parameters.name: camera
parameters.description: Defines the camera object.

examples.description: Set the camera object to BlizzardAllStars (default game camera)
Set the camera object to Dlft (default SC2 camera)

commands.description: Reset the game camera to `BlizzardAllStars` (default game camera)

examples.description: Reset the game camera to BlizzardAllStars
Reset the game camera to BlizzardAllStars

description: Force-run a trigger created by `TriggerCreate()`, ignoring conditions and waiting until it finishes.
This can be used to run a trigger that bypasses the `testConds` check, such as `GameCheatsEnabled(c_gameCheatCategoryDevelopment)`.
You can get the trigger name from the `TriggerCreate()` function in various galaxy files.
For example, a debug chat trigger to start the Immortal objective in Battlefield of Eternity immediately:

The trigger name will be `libMLBD_gt_MMBOEDebugStartImmediately_Func`. By using this command (`frt libMLBD_gt_MMBOEDebugStartImmediately_Func`), you can bypass the `GameCheatsEnabled(c_gameCheatCategoryDevelopment)` restriction, which was required when using the `-bd` command.

parameters.name: TriggerName
parameters.description: Defines the trigger to be run

examples.description: Force to run the trigger "libUIUI_gt_InputBackslashKeyDown_Func", which will show up the debug menu.
Force to run the trigger "libSprt_gt_DEBUGHaveAITakeOverAI_Func", which will have an AI take over your unit.

description: Toggle Alliance Control and Multi-Selection — a shortcut reimplementation of the QA Cheat Menu's `/ac` and `ts` commands.
This enables or disables "Alliance Control" (control units from other players, including enemies) and "Multi-Selection" (select multiple units at once, like Vikings and Samuro).

examples.description: Toggle Alliance Control + Multi-Selection
Toggle Alliance Control + Multi-Selection

description: Get or set the minion wave spawn interval. Usually 30s, or 15s in ARAM.

parameters.name: seconds
parameters.description: Defines interval for minion wave to spawn
parameters.defaultValue: (The map's original interval, usually 30 but 15 for ARAMs)

examples.description: Get the minion wave interval
Get the minion wave interval
Set the minion wave to spawn every 0.0625 seconds (each game loop)
Set the minion wave to spawn every 100 seconds.

description: End the game and instantly load a `.stormmap` file.
There are currently 4 built-in `.stormmaps` from CASC in Heroes, which the `MapPath` is:
You can also put a `.stormmap` file into `HEROES_INSTALL_LOCATION/maps/`, e.g `HEROES_INSTALL_LOCATION/maps/cool.stormmap`, which the `MapPath` will be `cool.stormmap`

parameters.name: MapPath
parameters.description: Defines the path to the stormmap

examples.description: Exit and Load the tutorial 1 map
Exit and Load the veteran tutorial map.

description: Show a custom floating combat text with different built-in templates.
The table is the available combinations of `state` and `type`. Columns are `state` (e.g `Shield`) and Rows are `type` (e.g `NormalAmountGiven`):
>Note: The capitalization of `type` must be followed exactly.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Defines the message to be shown in Floating Combat Text

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Defines the state of the Floating Combat Text
parameters.defaultValue: Basic

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Defines the type of the Floating Combat Text
parameters.defaultValue: NormalAmountGiven

examples.description: Shows the FCT "100" with the style of basic attack (orange)
Shows the FCT "50" with the style of xp gathered (small purple text)
Shows the FCT "nice" with the style of quest progressing (gold circle with "!")
Shows the FCT "999" with the style of critical shield (white with explosion style)

description: Useful for quickly displaying strings from internal functions.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Defines the mode of the string to be displayed.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Defines the value of the string to be displayed

examples.description: Shows the string "Set Mastery Ring..." from supportlib
Shows the string "Game Speed should not exceed 8.0...." from supportlib

description: Play a Cutscene file (`*.StormCutscene`) directly. Press `Esc` anytime to stop it.

The file path is relative to `*.stormmod/base.stormdata`. (e.g If Cutscene file is in `mods/heroesdata.stormmod/base.stormdata/Cutscenes/EndOfMatch/EndOfMatch_LineUp_Tassadar.StormCutscene`, the path will be `Cutscenes/EndOfMatch/EndOfMatch_LineUp_Tassadar.StormCutscene`)

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Defines the path of the *.StormCutscene file.

examples.description: Plays the Tutorial Cutscene for Cursed Hollow
Plays the Muradin Cutscene in the Collection Page (wait for 2 second to show Muradin)

description: The game speed must be between 0 and 4:

parameters.name: speed
parameters.description: Defines the game speed to be set (between 0 - 4).

examples.description: Set the game speed to 0 (Slower)
Set the game speed to 3 (Fast)
Reset the game speed to 2 (Normal).
Reset the game speed to 2 (Normal).

description: Toggle the Terrain Indicator (used by Diablo, Auriel, etc.) for any unit.

examples.description: Toggle Terrain Indicator
Toggle Terrain Indicator

_metadata.libraryDescription: A library with commands related to selected units, such as kill, spawn, etc.

commands.description: Get all units owned by a specific player.

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: Define which player to get all the units.
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Get all units owned by whoever uses this command.
Get all units owned by Player 7
Get all units owned by Player 15 (Player 15 usually are neutral mercenary camps.)

commands.description: Get all selected units from a specific player.

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: Define which player to get all the units.
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Get all selected units from whoever uses this command
Get all selected units from Player 6

commands.description: Instantly kill all selected unit(s).

examples.description: Instantly kill all selected unit(s).
Instantly kill all selected unit(s).

commands.description: Toggle unit ID display mode. When enabled, left-clicking a unit shows its ID on screen.

examples.description: Toggle displaying units' id mode.
Toggle displaying units' id mode.

description: Spawn unit(s) at the center of the camera.
>Note: Newly summoned units are automatically added to the selection group.

>Note: To control summoned units, enable multi-selection via the [`alliancecontrolteamselection`](#cmd-alliancecontrolteamselection) command or QA Cheat Menu (`ts`, and also `/ac` if the unit does not belong to you). They will behave like Vikings and Samuro with Illusion Master.
>Note: If your hero is Samuro, multi-selection may behave unexpectedly due to Blizzard's implementation.

parameters.name: UnitId
parameters.description: Define the unit to be spawned. You can get the id from <CUnit id="xxx"> in the game XML files.

parameters.name: Amount
parameters.description: Define the amount of unit(s) to be spawned

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: Define which player to own the unit that spawned.
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.description: Define scale of the unit(s) to be spawned. default size is 1.0

examples.description: Summon a tower structure to the player who used the command
Summon 3 Raynor Hero units for the player who used the command
Summon 2 Mal'Ganis Hero units for player 4
Summon 2 boss golems for player 1 with the unit scale to 0.5 (half the size)
Summon 10 Catapult Minions for player 5 with the unit scale to 2 (double the size)

description: Clear all summoned unit(s) (spawned via the [summon](#cmd-summon) command) for a player. Useful for removing large numbers of summoned units, or when summoned units are untargetable/unselectable and cannot be removed with the [killunits](#cmd-killunits) command.

>Note: Due to memory limitations, only the **last 500 summoned units** can be removed with this command. Older units will not be affected. To remove ALL summoned units, either respec your hero or restart the game.
>Self note: For each `MaxPlayer[16][100]`, is equivalent to around 30 `EventChatMessage` triggers. So more memory assigned to this, the less trigger can be used.

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: Define to clear which player's summoned unit(s).
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Clear summoned unit(s) from player who used the command
Clear summoned unit(s) from Player 6

description: Replace all selected units with the target unit.
>Note: Use with caution. Unexpected behavior may occur if the target unit does not exist or if a unit dies during the replacement. If this happens, use the debug menu to respawn your hero.

parameters.name: TargetUnitId
parameters.description: Define the selected units to be replaced with. You can get the id from <CUnit id="xxx"> in the game XML files.

examples.description: Replace all selected unit(s) to Raynor Hero.
Replace all selected unit(s) to Catapult Minion.

description: Show or modify a selected unit's property, such as max health, regen, movement speed, etc.
If `value` is not provided, the current property value is returned. If `value` is provided, the property is set accordingly.
The `type` parameters accept either the `Type (int)` or `Type (string)` below.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The property type to get or modify. See the table above.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to be set for the property type

examples.description: Shows the unit's current HP
Set the selected unit's current HP to 1.
Show the selected unit's Max Energy (the resource bar: mana, Tracer bullets, Chen's brew, etc.)
Set the selected unit's Max Energy to 10000 (the resource bar: mana, Tracer bullets, Chen's brew, etc.)
Shows the base movement speed of selected units.
Set the base movement speed of selected units to 10. Note that base speed for heroes is 4.8398

commands.description: List all active behaviors for selected units.

examples.description: List all active behaviors for selected units
List all active behaviors for selected units

commands.description: Clear all active behaviors for selected units.

examples.description: Clear all active behaviors for selected units
Clear all active behaviors for selected units

commands.description: Add a behavior to selected unit(s). Behaviors are generally found in `<CBehavior*>`, such as `<CBehaviorBuff id="xxx">` or `<CBehaviorAbility id="xxx">`.

parameters.name: BehaviorId
parameters.description: Defines the behavior id to be added to selected units

examples.description: Add Medivh's Arcane Brilliance Ability to selected units
Make all selected units permanent Invulnerable

description: Remove a behavior from selected unit(s). Behaviors are generally found in `<CBehavior*>`, such as `<CBehaviorBuff id="xxx">` or `<CBehaviorAbility id="xxx">`.
>Tip: Use the [`getbehavior`](#cmd-getbehavior) command to list active behaviors.

parameters.name: BehaviorId
parameters.description: Defines the behavior id to be removed from selected units

examples.description: Remove Medivh's Arcane Brilliance Ability from selected units
No longer makes all selected units permanent Invulnerable

commands.description: Add an effect to selected unit(s). Effects are generally found in `<CEffect*>`, such as `<CEffectSet id="xxxx">` or `<CEffectCreatePersistent id="xxx">`.

parameters.name: EffectId
parameters.description: Defines the effect id to be added to selected unit(s).

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Defines the amount of effects to be added to the units

examples.description: Kill all selected units (self-destruct)
Apply Medivh's Polybomb to all selected units
Make all selected units hit by Greymane's Cursed Bullet (damage) 5 times in a row.

commands.description: Add an effect to the center of the camera. Effects are generally found in `<CEffect*>`, such as `<CEffectSet id="xxxx">` or `<CEffectCreatePersistent id="xxx">`.

parameters.name: EffectId
parameters.description: Defines the effect id to be added to the center of the camera.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Defines the amount of effects to be added to the center of the camera.

parameters.name: PlayerID
parameters.description: The Player ID for the effect owner
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Add some Haunted Mines Skulls to camera location
Add Haunted Mines's boss root animation to camera location
Spawn 10 Xul's skeleton to camera location
Spawn 10 Xul's skeleton to camera location with the effect owner of Player 6

commands.description: Remove an effect from selected unit(s). Effects are generally found in `<CEffect*>`, such as `<CEffectSet id="xxxx">` or `<CEffectCreatePersistent id="xxx">`.

parameters.name: EffectId
parameters.description: Defines the effect id to be removed from selected unit(s).

examples.description: Remove Medivh's Polybomb effect from selected unit(s)

commands.description: Remove an effect from the center of the camera. Effects are generally found in `<CEffect*>`, such as `<CEffectSet id="xxxx">` or `<CEffectCreatePersistent id="xxx">`.

parameters.name: EffectId
parameters.description: Defines the effect id to be removed from the center of the camera.

examples.description: Remove Haunted Mines Skulls from camera location
Remove Haunted Mines's boss root animation from camera location

description: Play an Animation for selected unit(s).
>Note: There are Walk, Attack, A to Z animations etc

parameters.name: AnimationId
parameters.description: Defines the Animation ID to be played on selected unit(s)

examples.description: Play the Attack Animation for selected unit(s)
Play the Walk animation for selected unit(s)

description: Remove an Animation from selected unit(s).
>Note: There are Walk, Attack, A to Z animations etc

parameters.name: AnimationId
parameters.description: Defines the Animation ID to be removed from selected unit(s)

examples.description: Stop the Attack Animation for selected unit(s)
Stop the Walk animation for selected unit(s)

description: Send an Actor Message for selected unit(s). Generally from `<CActor*>`, the `<on send="xxx">`.
>Note: The command allows spaces for the parameter, since most actor messages have spaces.

parameters.name: ActorMessage
parameters.description: Defines the Actor Message to be sent. (This parameter allows spaces.)

examples.description: Play the A Spell animation, different on each hero
Hide the status bar, HP bar

commands.description: Gets the Ownership (Player ID) of selected unit(s).

examples.description: Gets the Ownership (Player ID) of selected unit(s).
Gets the Ownership (Player ID) of selected unit(s).

description: Changes ownership of the selected unit(s).
>Note: 0 is neutral; neutral units cannot be selected after their ownership is changed.

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: The new owner of the selected unit(s).

examples.description: Change ownership of all selected units to Player 1
Change all selected units's ownership to Player 6

commands.description: Continuously reset the weapon (auto attack) cooldown, effectively increasing attack speed.

examples.description: Continuously reset the weapon (auto attack) cooldown
Continuously reset the weapon (auto attack) cooldown

commands.description: Add a weapon (Basic Attack) to selected unit(s). Usually found in `<CWeapon* id='xxx'>`

parameters.description: The ID of the weapon

examples.description: Add Alexstrasza's Dragon Form weapon to selected unit(s)

commands.description: Remove a weapon (Basic Attack) from selected unit(s). Usually found in `<CWeapon* id='xxx'>`

parameters.name: WeaponID
parameters.description: The ID of the weapon

examples.description: Remove Ana's weapon from selected unit(s)
Remove D.va mech mode's weapon from selected unit(s)

commands.description: Get all weapons (Basic Attack) from selected unit(s). Usually found in `<CWeapon* id='xxx'>`

examples.description: Get all weapons from selected units
Get all weapons from selected units

commands.description: Remove all weapons (Basic Attack) from selected unit(s). Usually found in `<CWeapon* id='xxx'>`

examples.description: Remove all weapons from selected units
Remove all weapons from selected units

description: Kill all units owned by a specific player.
Unlike other commands that accept a player ID, this command also accepts Player 0 (Neutral).
>Note: In most maps, the Core is owned by Player 11 (left team) and Player 12 (right team). Killing it will instantly destroy the core and end the game.

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: Define which player to get all the units.
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Kill all units owned by whoever uses this command.
Kill all units owned by Player 7
Kill all units owned by Player 15 (Player 15 usually are neutral mercenary camps.)

description: >Note: This may conflict with non-collision units such as Medivh's Raven form. Avoid using this command on those units.

examples.description: Enable collision for selected unit(s).
Enable collision for selected unit(s).

description: >Note: This may conflict with non-collision units such as Medivh's Raven form. Avoid using this command on those units.

examples.description: Disable Collision for selected unit(s).
Disable Collision for selected unit(s).

_metadata.libraryDescription: Commands related to players, such as scoreboard, respawn timer, etc.

description: Add a specific amount of quest stacks to a player.

parameters.name: stacks
parameters.description: The amount of stack to be added for a player

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: The Player ID for the quest stacks to be added
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Add 10 quest stacks to whoever uses this command
Add 20 quest stacks to Player 6.

description: Remove a specific amount of quest stacks from a player.

parameters.name: stacks
parameters.description: The amount of stack to be removed from a player

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: The Player ID for the quest stacks to be removed
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Remove 10 quest stacks from whoever uses this command
Remove 20 quest stacks from Player 6.

description: Toggle mass quest completion mode, granting stacks every game tick (0.0625s) for whoever uses this command.

>Note: Useful for heroes with truly infinite stacking such as Zul'jin or Nazeebo. However, due to integer/floating-point overflow, after reaching a certain stack count the functionality may overflow — for example, Nazeebo's HP may reduce to 1 when at maximum stacks with the level 4 bonus HP-per-stack talent.

parameters.name: stacks
parameters.description: The amount of stacks to add every game tick (0.0625s)

examples.description: Enable mass quest completion mode and give 10 stacks every game tick if not enabled
Disable mass quest completion mode if enabled

description: Set the Mastery Ring level. The ring below the hero will be automatically updated.
>Note: If the new level is lower than the current one, you may need to respawn (e.g., suicide) the selected units.

parameters.name: MasteryRingLevel
parameters.description: The level of the mastery ring to be set.

examples.description: Remove the mastery ring from the player
Set the mastery ring to Level 3.

commands.description: Toggle the display of the talents selection tree.

examples.description: Toggle the display of the talents selection tree.
Toggle the display of the talents selection tree.

commands.description: Toggle whether to pause the game when viewing talents, similar to heroes tutorial.

examples.description: Toggle whether to pause the game when viewing talents
Toggle whether to pause the game when viewing talents

description: Add a talent to a player, generally found in `<CTalent id="xxxx">`.
>Note: Some of the talents won't work, so you might want to experiment a bit.

parameters.name: TalentId
parameters.description: The talent id to be added

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: The target player id to have the talent being added.
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Add Medivh's Arcane Explosion Talent to whoever uses this command
add Malfurion's Celestial Alignment Talent to Player 2

description: Add all talents to a player.
The `HeroId` can be either the ID of `<CHero id="xxx">`, which will add all of the talents from that hero to the player, or it can be `ALL`, which will add ALL the talents in the game to the player.
>Note: Some of the talents won't work, so you might want to experiment a bit.

parameters.name: HeroId
parameters.description: The talents from the Hero / all the talents in the game to be added from a player

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: The target player id to have the talent being added.
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Add all the talents from Tyrande to the player whoever uses the command
Add all the talents from Blaze to Player 2
Add ALL the talents from the game to the player whoever uses the command
Add ALL the talents from the game to the player 10

description: Select all talents for a player. Ever wonder what a Q and AA build Zeratul at the same time would feel like? Or a Varian with Twin Blade+Smash+Taunt and triple banners?

>Note: This uses the built-in `libGame_gf_TalentsChooseTalentForPlayer()` function, which adds talents for the player's current hero only. Summoned hero units do not count. Unlike the `addalltalent` command, this works reliably most of the time.

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: The target player id to have the talent being selected.
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Select all the talents for player whoever uses the command
Select all the talents for player whoever uses the command
Select all the talents for player 6
Select all the talents for player 6

description: Remove a talent from a player, generally found in `<CTalent id="xxxx">`.
>Note: Some of the talents won't work, so you might want to experiment a bit.

parameters.name: TalentId
parameters.description: The talent id to be removed

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: The target player id to have the talent being removed.
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Remove Medivh's Arcane Explosion Talent from whoever uses this command
Remove Malfurion's Celestial Alignment Talent from Player 2

description: Remove all talents from a player.

The `HeroId` can be either the ID of `<CHero id="xxx">`, which will remove all of the talents from that hero from the player, or it can be `ALL`, which will remove ALL the talents in the game from the player.
>Note: Some of the talents won't work, so you might want to experiment a bit.

parameters.name: HeroId
parameters.description: The talents from the Hero / all the talents in the game to be removed from a player

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: The target player ID from which to remove the talent.
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Remove all the talents from Tyrande from the player whoever uses the command
Remove all the talents from Blaze from Player 2
Remove ALL the talents from the game from the player whoever uses the command
Remove ALL the talents from the game from Player 10

commands.description: Enable a disabled talent for all players. Generally found in `<CTalent id="xxxx">`.

parameters.name: TalentId
parameters.description: The talent id to be enabled.

examples.description: Enable Rag's Lava Wave talent, which was disabled in ARAM.
Enable Maiev's Pursuit Of Vengeance talent

description: Disable a talent for all players. Generally found in `<CTalent id="xxxx">`.
>Note: This only locks the talent selection. If a player has already taken the talent, it will not be affected.

parameters.name: TalentId
parameters.description: The talent id to be disabled.

examples.description: Disable Rag's Lava Wave talent, similar to ARAM.
Disable Maiev's Pursuit Of Vengeance talent

description: Override the current respawn timer for all players.
>Note: Due to system limitations, setting the value to 0 will be automatically changed to 0.0625 (1 game tick).

parameters.name: seconds
parameters.description: The respawn time to be set for all players

examples.description: Set the respawn timer to 99 seconds
Set the respawn timer to 0.0625 seconds (1 game tick)

description: Set the score for the scoreboard (some values are visible on the Tab screen). Useful when testing talents that interact with scoreboard values, such as Zagara's Infest.
>Note: The Infest internal function is capped at `524%` (maximum fixed value); further details are out of scope here.
You can obtain these FIELDs from `ScoreValueData.xml` in `heroesdata.stormmod`

>Note: Some fields uses Integer and some uses Fixed. However because this function is unified using `PlayerScoreValueSetFromInt()`, all decimals will be removed. As such, the max value for fixed is `524287` and `2147483647` for Integer. This limitation might change later.

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: The field to be modified in the scoreboard.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to be set for the field in the scoreboard.

parameters.name: PlayerId
parameters.description: The player id for the modification to be applied.
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Set the field "Hero Damage" to 0 for the player who use this command.
Set the field "Kills" to 5242873 for the player who use this command
Set the field "Healing" to 1000 for the player 2

commands.description: Modify the respawn point for a player. Either at the current camera location or its default location.

parameters.name: Action
parameters.description: Choose the action of the command. (set = set the respawn location at camera location, reset = reset all respawn location)

parameters.name: PlayerId/all
parameters.description: Set the player id for the spawn modification to take effect, use "all" for all players.
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Set the respawn point for all players at the current camera location
Set the respawn point to the current camera location for the player who uses the command
Set the respawn point for player 6 at the current camera location
Reset player 2's respawn point to the default location
Reset all players' respawn points to their default locations (Hall of Storms).

parameters.name: OverlayPath
parameters.description: The path of the overlay file, relative to base.stormdata.

Set the overlay to Stitches Gorge.

description: Directly get a catalog value (i.e., XML data) for a player.
The `CatalogReference` string consists of three parts: `<Category Type>,<Category Entry>,<Catalog Field Path>` separated by commas, where:
- `<Category Type>`: The type of the catalog, which is usually the second word of the entry. Take `CBehaviorBuff` as an example — it consists of three parts:
- The value is the second "Capitalised Word": `Behavior`. Similarly, `CAbilEffectInstant`, the value will be `Abil`, `CValidatorPlayerTalent` will be `Validator`.
- `<Category Entry>`: The ID of the target Category. (`id="xxx"`)
- `<Catalog Field Path>`: The field path of the targeted `<Category Entry>`.
- Nested levels use `.` for representation. If the target field is not `value="xxx"`, it must also be nested.
- Arrays uses `[n]` to specify the numeric order
- If an entry can exist multiple times with different index such as `<Flags>`, use `[Index]`, such as `Flags[AllowSelfCast]`
Therefore, consider the following XML (extracted from `wizarddata.xml`, Li ming's data file):
To get a reference to her basic attack range, the `CatalogReference` string will be `Weapon,HeroWizardWeapon,Range`
Another Example: (Extracted from `firebat.xml`, Blaze's Data file)
To get a reference to Blaze's Jet Propulsion (E) cooldown, the `CatalogReference` will be `Abil,FirebatJetPropulsion,Cost.Cooldown.TimeUse`
(Extracted from `stukovdata.xml`, Stukov's data file — the `Biotic Armor` talent, which grants 15 Armor to allies affected by Healing Pathogen)

To get a reference to the armor value against Structures, the `CatalogReference` will be `Behavior,StukovHealingPathogenBioticArmorPhysicalArmorBehavior,ArmorModification.ArmorSet[Structure].ArmorMitigationTable[Basic]`.

parameters.name: CatalogReference
parameters.description: The Reference to the specific catalog field

parameters.name: PlayerID
parameters.description: The Player ID for the catalog modification
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Get Li-Ming's Calamity damage from whoever uses this command
Get Varian's Charge mana cost from whoever uses this command
Get the mount speed for all Heroes from Player 2
Get Stukov's Basic Attack speed from whoever uses this command
Get Chromie's Basic Attack Effect from whoever uses this command
Get Dehaka's Dark Swarm Ability Suppress Collision flag value when active from Player 2

description: Directly modify a Catalog value (a.k.a XMLs) for a player in runtime.
For how to obtain and construct `CatalogReference`, Please refer to the [`getcatalog`](#cmd-getcatalog-description) command.

- Some of the fields are READ-ONLY, meaning it cannot be modified, e.g `Unit,HeroChromie,PlaneArray[Air]` (Unable to make Chromie become an air unit like Medivh Raven). This would require an override to the XML instead (see [modding.md](modding.md)).

- The catalog modification is applied after parental inheritance, meaning it is not possible to modify a parent (`parent="xxx"`) and have the change apply to all child catalogs. You must apply it to each child catalog individually.
- It is not possible to "create" a value that does not exist after the map has loaded. Only existing values (after inheriting from a parent) can be modified.

parameters.name: CatalogReference
parameters.description: The Reference to the catalog field to be modified

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to be set for the specified catalog field

parameters.name: PlayerID
parameters.description: The Player ID for the catalog modification
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Modify Li-Ming's Calamity damage to 9999 for whoever uses this command
Modify Varian's Charge mana cost to 5 for whoever uses this command
Modify the mount speed for all Heroes to -30% (30% slower instead of faster) for Player 2
Modify Stukov's Basic Attack speed to 16 (1 second performs 16 attacks = 0.0625 period per AA) for whoever uses this command
Modify Chromie's Basic Attack to behave like Li-ming's Basic Attack (Shooting a Purple Missile) for whoever uses this command
Modify Dehaka's Dark Swarm to no longer suppress collision when active (units can no longer pass through each other) for Player 2

description: Directly modify a catalog value (i.e., XML data) for a player at runtime.
For how to obtain and construct `CatalogReference`, please refer to the [`getcatalog`](#cmd-getcatalog-description) command.

This is similar to the [`modifycatalog`](#cmd-modifycatalog-description) command, but uses the `CatalogFieldValueSet()` function directly. If the regular `modifycatalog` command does not work, try this one.
> Note: This command format is a bit different from the `modifycatalog` command, as it does not require the `CatalogReference` to be separated by comma. Instead, it is separated by space.
> Note: due to unknown reason, `<CItemClass>` and `<CItemContainer>` currently does not work with this command.

parameters.name: CatalogType
parameters.description: The Catalog Type to be modified

parameters.name: CatalogEntry
parameters.description: The Catalog Entry to be modified

parameters.name: CatalogField
parameters.description: The Catalog Field to be modified

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to be set for the specified catalog field

parameters.name: PlayerID
parameters.description: The Player ID for the catalog modification
parameters.defaultValue: The player id of whoever used this command.

examples.description: Modify Li-Ming's Calamity damage to 9999 for whoever uses this command
Modify Varian's Charge mana cost to 5 for whoever uses this command
Modify the mount speed for all Heroes to -30% (30% slower instead of faster) for Player 2
Modify Stukov's Basic Attack speed to 16 (1 second performs 16 attacks = 0.0625 period per AA) for whoever uses this command
Modify Chromie's Basic Attack to behave like Li-ming's Basic Attack (Shooting a Purple Missile) for whoever uses this command
Modify Dehaka's Dark Swarm to no longer suppress collision when active (units can no longer pass through each other) for Player 2

_metadata.libraryDescription: A Builder Library that allows you to place objects anywhere in the map.
overrideMarkdownContent: **This system is still in early development.**

Builder mode lets you place objects anywhere on the map. Units will continuously teleport to your cursor (following it). Remember to select `/ac` and `ts` in the QA menu (these will be added automatically in a future update).
To use builder mode, select a unit, or units and press `backspace`. Builder mode will then be activated and you can place the units where you like.
To place the unit(s), press `backspace` again to deactivate builder mode.

_metadata.libraryDescription: A First Person Shooter like Library that allows you to see the map in first person camera.
overrideMarkdownContent: **This system is still in early development.**
FPS Mode lets you view the map through a first-person camera attached to your unit. The camera continuously looks forward and rotates while the mode is active.
To toggle FPS mode, simply use the `fps` chat command.
- `u` : Move your unit forward. (It will also clear queue commands and **Hold Position** after moved.)
- `← (arrow key)` : Turn your unit and camera to left.
- `→ (arrow key)` : Turn your unit and camera to right.

_metadata.libraryDescription: Free Camera Mode allows you to change the angle of the camera easily.
overrideMarkdownContent: **This system is still in early development.**
Free Camera Mode lets you use the `Middle Mouse Wheel` to freely change your camera angle and direction.
To enable or disable, simply use the chat command `tfc` or `togglefreecamera`!
>Note: Camera pan (move the camera by pushing mouse to the edge) will be disabled when free camera mode is active

_metadata.libraryDescription: Random useless stuff just for the lolz.
